headline: Semiconductors Crater as Healthcare Surges to Session Lead
body_html:
Wednesday's midday tape is a study in extremes. With the session still unresolved, the 198-name universe sits nearly split — 98 issues higher against 100 lower — yet the aggregate mood is unmistakably skewed by a violent semiconductor selloff. The chip-equipment and memory complex is the dominant story, dragging the Semiconductors sector down 7.3% at midday and pulling Materials and Industrials into the red as well.

Against that backdrop, Healthcare has emerged as the clear defensive anchor, rising 2.53% to lead all sectors. Consumer names are also holding ground, up 1.38%, while Software and Homebuilders add fractional gains. The Financial-Data space is punching above its weight: S&P Global and Equifax both climbed more than 5%, with Fair Isaac adding 4.5%, suggesting investors are rotating toward businesses tied to credit analytics and data licensing amid the uncertainty.

Into the close, the key watch is whether the semiconductor rout deepens or stabilizes. Sandisk leads decliners at nearly -15%, trailed closely by Teradyne and KLA Corp, both off roughly 13.6%. Any relief bounce in that cohort could reshape the session's final complexion considerably.

briefs.headline: Financial-Data Firms Claim Three of Six Top Spots on the Leaderboard
briefs.body: S&P Global, Equifax, and Fair Isaac are collectively pacing the session's gainers, with advances of 5.35%, 5.06%, and 4.50% respectively. The trio's shared strength points to a bid for businesses built on proprietary data and credit-scoring infrastructure — a corner of the market insulated from the semiconductor-driven turbulence dominating headlines today.

briefs.headline: Roche and Intuitive Surgical Anchor Healthcare's Sector-Leading Advance
briefs.body: Swiss pharma giant Roche climbed 4.85% in midday trading, while robotic-surgery specialist Intuitive Surgical matched it almost exactly at 4.81%. Together they underscore why Healthcare is the session's top-performing sector, up 2.53%, as investors seek shelter in companies with durable demand profiles and limited direct exposure to trade-sensitive supply chains.

briefs.headline: Autodesk Breaks Above Software Peers With Nearly 5% Intraday Gain
briefs.body: Design-software firm Autodesk is outrunning the broader Software sector — which is only marginally positive on the day — with a 4.81% advance at midday. The move makes it one of just a handful of names in the universe posting gains above 4%, a distinction it shares exclusively with the Financial-Data cluster and the healthcare names atop today's tape.

briefs.headline: Memory and Storage Names Bear the Brunt of Chip-Sector Collapse
briefs.body: Sandisk is the session's sharpest decliner at -14.93%, with Western Digital off 11.34% — together painting a bleak picture for the memory and storage sub-segment. The synchronized selloff suggests a sector-wide reassessment rather than company-specific news, and positions both names as the most acute pressure points heading into the afternoon.

briefs.headline: UBS Data Underscores How 2025 Equity Gains Minted Nearly a Million New Millionaires
briefs.body: A newly released UBS report finds that last year's sustained stock-market advance pushed roughly one million additional households into millionaire status. The finding arrives as the current session's gains remain concentrated in a narrow band of sectors and names, prompting renewed debate about how broadly market wealth is being distributed across the investing public.

briefs.headline: Jeff Bezos Family Office Makes a June Push Into Artificial-Intelligence Ventures
briefs.body: The family office associated with Jeff Bezos backed five AI-focused startups during June alone, according to disclosures reviewed by financial media. The activity signals continued appetite among ultra-high-net-worth investors for early-stage AI exposure even as publicly traded semiconductor and chip-equipment stocks — often seen as the picks-and-shovels of AI infrastructure — are under severe pressure in today's session.
